topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring splash of blue on my otherwise dull screen. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My heart gave a strange, uneven thump against my ribs. I hadn't even thought about him all afternoon, just staring blankly at the quadratic equations. Hesitantly, I clicked it open. The message was a picture of a very enthusiastic, slightly blurry photo of the school library, captioned simply: "Quiet study session vibes. 📚". A wave of awkwardness washed over me, quickly followed by a peculiar sort of warmth. He always seemed so dedicated, so invested in the subject, and now this—a glimpse into his personal space, shared online. I scrolled through his recent posts, noticing the subtle changes in his tone, the way he seemed to engage with other students in a way that felt… more personal than just classroom instruction. I felt a strange pull, a recognition that went beyond the syllabus.

[story 2]

I was cleaning out my old tablet when I stumbled across it. A profile picture of Ms. Evans, my English teacher, looking relaxed in a garden setting. Then I saw the timeline. She had posted a thoughtful critique of a novel I’d read, a comment that was surprisingly insightful and warm. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. It wasn't just that she was online; it was the intimacy of the shared space. I reread her caption, a small, involuntary smile tugging at the corner of my mouth. It felt like uncovering a hidden layer of someone I admired from afar. A deep sense of connection settled in my chest, heavy and unfamiliar. I spent a good ten minutes just watching the photos, trying to process the sudden shift in how I perceived her presence in my life.

[story 3]

It happened during a late-night scroll through Instagram. I was half-asleep, the blue light painting shadows on my ceiling, when I saw it: Mr. Davies tagging me in a story about a school event. His caption was brief—a simple thumbs-up emoji. My breath hitched. I sat bolt upright, the sudden jolt making my hands shake. It wasn't an accusation, or even a grand gesture; it was just a quiet acknowledgment, a small piece of his attention directed straight at me. A quiet sense of validation bloomed in my stomach, surprising me with its gentle strength. I stared at the screen, feeling a profound sense of being seen, of being noticed in a way that felt incredibly meaningful.
